title: Mock Test 07 — Mine Surveying & Mineral Exploration

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the antonym of 'TRANSPARENT'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Transparent' means allowing light to pass through so objects are visible.

**Working.** The opposite is 'opaque' (not allowing light through); the other options are near-synonyms of transparent.

**Answer.** opaque.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The bar chart shows four values: $10, 20, 30, 40$. Compute their total.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='100' width='25' height='20'/><rect x='85' y='80' width='25' height='40'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='40' width='25' height='80'/></g><text x='50' y='138' font-size='8'>10, 20, 30, 40</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An article costing $200$ is sold for $250$. The profit percentage is _____ % .

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the word most similar in meaning to 'ABATE'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Abate' means to become less intense or to reduce.

**Working.** Among the options, 'diminish' is the closest synonym; 'increase' is its antonym.

**Answer.** diminish.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A train $200$ m long moving at $36$ km/h crosses a platform $100$ m long in _____ seconds.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** To cross a platform, the train covers (its length + platform length).

**Working.** Speed $=36\times\tfrac{5}{18}=10$ m/s; distance $=200+100=300$ m; time $=\dfrac{300}{10}=30$ s.

**Answer.** $30$ s.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a class, B is $5$th from the top and $12$th from the bottom in marks. The total number of students is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total $=$ rank from top $+$ rank from bottom $-1$.

**Working.** $5+12-1=16$.

**Answer.** $16$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Using A=1, B=2, ..., Z=26, the sum of the letter values of the word 'ORE' is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Replace each letter with its position value and add.

**Working.** O$=15$, R$=18$, E$=5$; sum $=38$.

**Answer.** $38$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A train $150$ m long moving at $54$ km/h crosses a stationary pole in _____ seconds.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** To cross a pole, the train covers its own length.

**Working.** $54$ km/h $=54\times\tfrac{5}{18}=15$ m/s; time $=\dfrac{150}{15}=10$ s.

**Answer.** $10$ s.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Two fair dice are rolled. The probability that their sum is $8$ is _____ (round to two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count favourable pairs out of $36$.

**Working.** Sum $8$: $(2,6),(3,5),(4,4),(5,3),(6,2)=5$ ways; $\dfrac{5}{36}\approx 0.14$.

**Answer.** $\approx 0.14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Read the passage and answer.

'Acid mine drainage occurs when sulphide minerals react with water and oxygen. The resulting acidic water can dissolve heavy metals, contaminating nearby streams long after a mine has closed.'

According to the passage, acid mine drainage:
options: can continue to pollute water even after mining stops
occurs only while a mine is operating
is caused by the complete absence of oxygen
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Match the option to the explicit wording of the passage.

**Working.** The text states contamination persists 'long after a mine has closed', so pollution can continue post-closure. Oxygen is required (not absent), and metals are dissolved (not neutralised).

**Answer.** can continue to pollute water even after mining stops.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: Using the declining-balance method at a rate of $20\%$ per year on an asset costing $\textsf{Rs}\,500{,}000$, compute the book value at the end of year $1$ ($\textsf{Rs}$).

figure.caption: Declining-balance depreciation
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='220' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M30 30 Q90 95 210 112' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><text x='100' y='140' font-size='8'>declining balance</text></svg>

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Random (accidental) errors in a set of observations are best reduced by
options: taking many observations and using their mean, since they tend to cancel
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Random errors obey the laws of probability.

**Reasoning.** Being equally likely positive or negative and small, they partly cancel; the mean of many observations is more precise, with the standard error decreasing as $1/\sqrt{n}$.

**Answer.** Average many observations.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: The factor $(F/P,\,i,\,n)=(1+i)^n$ is called the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** It converts a present single sum to its future value.

**Working.** $(F/P)=(1+i)^n$ is the single-payment compound-amount factor.

**Answer.** single-payment compound-amount factor.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Hydraulic/paste backfill in a metal mine is valued because it
options: raises the ore grade in situ
improves ground stability, disposes of tailings underground and permits higher overall extraction
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fill is both a structural and an environmental tool.

**Reasoning.** Placed in mined voids, fill confines walls and adjacent pillars (allowing them to be recovered), controls subsidence, and provides a route to dispose of mill tailings (especially as paste) underground.

**Answer.** Stability, tailings disposal and higher extraction.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: The 'specific drilling' (drill factor) of a blast is defined as
options: the metres drilled per cubic metre (or tonne) of rock blasted
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Specific drilling normalises drilling effort to broken rock.

**Reasoning.** It is the total drilled length divided by the volume (or tonnage) of rock the round breaks — an index of drilling efficiency that complements the powder factor.

**Answer.** Metres drilled per m³ (or tonne) blasted.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For a $3\times3$ matrix $A$ with $\det(A)=4$, the value of $\det(2A)$ is _____ .

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: In the balanced (two-cage) winder shown, the cage masses balance each other. Compute the out-of-balance force (kN) due to a 10 t payload alone (g = 9.81 m/s²).

figure.caption: Balanced winder
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='110' cy='35' r='16' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='95' y1='45' x2='95' y2='150' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='125' y1='45' x2='125' y2='150' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='80' y='150' width='30' height='28' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='112' y='120' width='28' height='26' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><text x='52' y='196' font-size='8'>balanced winder, payload 10 t</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** With cages balanced, only the payload weight is unbalanced.

**Working.**
$$F = 10000 \times 9.81 = 98100\ \text{N} = 98.1\ \text{kN}$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{98.1}$ kN.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A slurry (watergel) explosive is
options: a dry powder with no water
a thickened/gelled aqueous solution of ammonium nitrate (often with sensitisers), water resistant
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Slurries are gelled aqueous AN.

**Reasoning.** A cross-linked gel holds the AN solution and sensitisers; the gel resists water, so slurries (watergels) suit wet holes, though emulsions have now largely superseded them.

**Answer.** Gelled aqueous AN solution (water resistant).

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Given $\sigma_c=20\,\text{MPa}$ and $N=3$, the cohesion $c=\dfrac{\sigma_c}{2\sqrt{N}}$ is _____ MPa. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: mine-surveying
questions.stem: Two cross-sections $30$ m apart have areas $A_1 = 100$ m$^2$ and $A_2 = 140$ m$^2$. Using the end-area (average-area) method, compute the volume (m$^3$).

figure.caption: End-area volume between sections

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: With $D=10{,}000$ units/year and $\text{EOQ}=500$ units, the number of orders placed per year is _____ .

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A flat seam lies at depth $H = 200\ \text{m}$. With an angle of draw of $35^\circ$ (measured from the vertical), compute the horizontal distance (m) from a panel edge to the limit of surface subsidence.

figure.caption: Angle of draw over a panel edge
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='20' y1='40' x2='220' y2='40' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='90' y='120' width='60' height='14' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='90' y1='120' x2='55' y2='40' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><line x1='90' y1='120' x2='90' y2='40' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-dasharray='2 2'/><text x='95' y='82' font-size='8'>H=200 m</text><text x='30' y='150' font-size='8'>angle of draw=35 deg</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** The subsidence limit is offset horizontally by $H\tan(\text{angle of draw})$.

**Working.**
$$x=200\tan35^\circ=200\times0.7002=140.04\ \text{m}$$

**Answer.** $\approx 140\ \text{m}$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A seam is reported to have an incubation period of 8 weeks. Expressed in days (1 week = 7 days), this is ____ days.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: An equation of the form $\dfrac{dy}{dx}+P(x)\,y=Q(x)$ is a
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Linearity in $y$ and $y'$ with first derivative only.

**Working.** This standard form is a first-order linear ODE, solved by an integrating factor.

**Answer.** Linear first-order ODE.

questions.stem: A room-and-pillar layout has square pillars $10\ \text{m}\times10\ \text{m}$ on $20\ \text{m}\times20\ \text{m}$ centres (figure). Compute the extraction ratio.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: In the Maclaurin (Taylor about $0$) series of $e^{x}$, the coefficient of $x^{3}$ is

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A 3.5 m high roadway undergoes 7% vertical convergence. The roof-to-floor closure, in mm, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Closure $=$ convergence fraction $\times$ original height.

**Working.**
$$\Delta=0.07\times3500=245\ \text{mm}$$

**Answer.** 245 mm.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Capital Recovery
questions.stem: A loan of $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}00{,}000$ at $10\%$ is repaid by $5$ equal year-end payments. Each payment is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: For an activity with optimistic $t_o=4$, most-likely $t_m=6$ and pessimistic $t_p=8$ (days), the PERT expected time is _____ days.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The pressure required to pass $40\,\text{m}^3$/s through a network of equivalent resistance $0.5\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ is _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: The lower explosive limit of a cloud of fine coal dust in air is of the order of ____ g/m³.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A minimum dust concentration is needed to propagate an explosion.

**Reasoning.** Fine bituminous coal dust becomes explosible above roughly 50 g/m³ of air; below this the cloud is too lean to sustain flame propagation.

**Answer.** ~50 g/m³.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Barton's $Q=\dfrac{\text{RQD}}{J_n}\cdot\dfrac{J_r}{J_a}\cdot\dfrac{J_w}{\text{SRF}}$. For RQD $=90$, $J_n=9$, $J_r=3$, $J_a=1$, $J_w=1$, SRF $=1$, the value of $Q$ is _____ .

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: A simple circular curve has radius 200 m and deflection angle 60°. The tangent length, in m, is ____ (two decimals).

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: Cut-and-fill stoping mines the ore
options: in a single large open void left permanently
in successive (usually horizontal) slices, each mined slice being replaced with backfill that supports the walls and forms the next working floor
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Cut-and-fill alternates extraction with filling.

**Reasoning.** A slice of ore is mined, then the void is filled (hydraulic/paste/rock fill); the fill supports the walls, controls subsidence and gives a platform to mine the next slice. It suits weak walls and high-grade, irregular orebodies.

**Answer.** Slice-by-slice mining with backfill between slices.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Which of the following functions are continuous for ALL real $x$?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Identify points of discontinuity.

**Working.**
- $e^{x}$ — continuous everywhere (TRUE).
- $|x|$ — continuous everywhere (TRUE).
- $1/x$ — undefined at $x=0$ (FALSE).
- $x^{2}$ — polynomial, continuous everywhere (TRUE).

**Answer.** Options (a), (b) and (d).

questions.stem: For the stress element shown ($\sigma_x=40$, $\sigma_y=20$, $\tau_{xy}=10\ \mathrm{MPa}$), the major principal stress, in MPa, is _____ (two decimals).

questions.stem: A Brazilian disc of diameter $D=50\ \text{mm}$ and thickness $L=50\ \text{mm}$ splits at a load $P=15\ \text{kN}$. Compute the indirect tensile strength (MPa).

questions.stem: Two parallel branches share a common pressure drop of $400\,\text{Pa}$. The branch of resistance $4\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ carries _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Two similar matrices ($B=P^{-1}AP$) share all of the following EXCEPT
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Similarity preserves the characteristic polynomial (hence determinant, trace, eigenvalues, rank) but not the eigenvectors.

**Working.** If $Av=\lambda v$ then $B(P^{-1}v)=\lambda(P^{-1}v)$, so eigenvectors are transformed by $P^{-1}$ and differ in general.

**Answer.** Eigenvectors.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: In the direct shear test shown, the joint has cohesion $c=5\ \mathrm{MPa}$ and friction angle $\phi=30^\circ$, under a normal stress $\sigma_n=20\ \mathrm{MPa}$. The shear strength ($c+\sigma_n\tan\phi$), in MPa, is _____ (two decimals).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='70' width='120' height='30' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='40' y='100' width='120' height='30' fill='#d6d3d1' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'><line x1='80' y1='40' x2='80' y2='66' marker-end='url(#sn)'/><line x1='120' y1='40' x2='120' y2='66' marker-end='url(#sn)'/></g><line x1='160' y1='85' x2='200' y2='85' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#ss)'/><defs><marker id='sn' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='6' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L4 6 L8 0 Z' fill='#dc2626'/></marker><marker id='ss' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='84' y='32' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626'>sn=20</text><text x='40' y='145' font-size='9'>c=5 MPa, phi=30°</text></svg>
figure.caption: Direct shear box

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock has porosity $n=0.20$. Its void ratio is _____ .

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: A rope shovel has a dipper of rated (struck) capacity 5 m³ and works at a fill factor of 0.9. The loose volume excavated per dipper pass, in m³, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Actual loose load per pass $=$ rated capacity $\times$ fill factor.

**Working.**
$$V=5\times0.9=4.5\ \text{m}^3$$

**Answer.** 4.5 m³ (loose).

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $z=x^{2}y$, the estimated change $dz$ at $(2,3)$ when $dx=0.01$ and $dy=0.02$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total differential $dz=\dfrac{\partial z}{\partial x}dx+\dfrac{\partial z}{\partial y}dy$.

**Working.**
$$dz=2xy\,dx+x^{2}\,dy=2(2)(3)(0.01)+(4)(0.02)=0.12+0.08=0.20$$

**Answer.** $0.20$.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Rolle's theorem applies to $f$ on $[a,b]$ provided $f$ is continuous on $[a,b]$, differentiable on $(a,b)$, and

figure.caption: Rolle's theorem: f(a) = f(b) implies a point c in (a, b) with a horizontal tangent.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='20' y1='150' x2='220' y2='150' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><path d='M40 130 Q130 30 220 130' fill='none' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='90' y1='55' x2='170' y2='55' stroke='#ef4444' stroke-width='1.5' stroke-dasharray='4 3'/><circle cx='130' cy='52' r='3' fill='#ef4444'/><line x1='40' y1='150' x2='40' y2='130' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><line x1='220' y1='150' x2='220' y2='130' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='3 3'/><text x='34' y='165' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>a</text><text x='216' y='165' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>b</text><text x='124' y='165' font-size='10' fill='#ef4444'>c</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Hypotheses of Rolle's theorem.

**Working.** The extra equal-endpoint condition $f(a)=f(b)$ guarantees a point $c\in(a,b)$ with $f'(c)=0$.

**Answer.** $f(a)=f(b)$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: A small air leakage into a sealed fire area is dangerous because it can
options: supply just enough oxygen to keep the fire smouldering and may bring the gas mixture into the explosive range
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Partial oxygen supply is the worst case.

**Reasoning.** A small leak feeds slow combustion (heat and CO continue) without flushing the area, and can dilute the rich gas behind the seal into the explosive band — hence seals must be as airtight as possible.

**Answer.** Sustains smouldering and risks an explosive mixture.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: A magnetic survey is particularly useful for exploring
options: magnetite-bearing iron ore bodies, which produce magnetic anomalies
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Magnetic surveys map susceptibility contrasts.

**Reasoning.** Strongly magnetic minerals (magnetite) distort the earth's field, giving anomalies that outline iron ore (and mafic/ultramafic bodies); weakly magnetic rocks give little response.

**Answer.** Magnetite iron ore.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A plane element carries $\sigma_x=60\ \text{MPa}$, $\sigma_y=20\ \text{MPa}$ and zero shear. From the stress block, compute the maximum in-plane shear stress (MPa).

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways of equal resistance $R_1=R_2=12\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$ are connected in parallel (see figure). The equivalent resistance, in $\mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$, is _____.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: From the cost-revenue chart, fixed cost is $\textsf{Rs}\,100{,}000$/period, selling price is $\textsf{Rs}\,50$/t and variable cost is $\textsf{Rs}\,30$/t. Compute the break-even output (t).

figure.caption: Cost-revenue break-even chart

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem:
Assuming gravitational loading with no lateral strain, compute the ratio of horizontal to vertical stress $K=\dfrac{
u}{1-
u}$ for a rock with Poisson's ratio $
u=0.25$.

figure.caption: Confined element
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='65' y='55' width='50' height='50' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='90' y1='30' x2='90' y2='53' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#k)'/><line x1='35' y1='80' x2='63' y2='80' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#k)'/><defs><marker id='k' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='98' y='42' font-size='8'>sigma_v</text><text x='18' y='100' font-size='8'>sigma_h (no lateral strain)</text></svg>

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: Simpson's one-third rule for area requires that the number of divisions (intervals) be
options: even, with equal spacing (i.e. an odd number of ordinates)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Parabolic fitting pairs the strips.

**Reasoning.** The rule fits a parabola over each pair of strips, so the number of intervals must be even (ordinates odd) and the spacing equal; otherwise the last strip is handled separately.

**Answer.** Even number of equal intervals.

questions.stem: Offsets $5, 7, 9, 7, 5$ m are taken at a regular interval of $10$ m along a baseline. Using the trapezoidal rule, compute the area (m$^2$).

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: An orebody holds 2 Mt in situ; with partial pillar recovery on retreat the overall recovery is 70%. The recoverable ore, in Mt, is ____ (one decimal).

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A project costs $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}00{,}000$, returns $\textsf{Rs}\,30{,}000$/year for $5$ years and has a salvage of $\textsf{Rs}\,20{,}000$ at year $5$. At $10\%$, its NPV is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ . Round to the nearest rupee.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the discounted salvage to the annuity NPV.

**Working.**
$$-100000+30000(3.7908)+\frac{20000}{1.61051}=-100000+113724+12418=26142.$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,26{,}142$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan absorbs $20\,\text{kW}$ at $600\,\text{rpm}$. At $720\,\text{rpm}$ it absorbs _____ kW. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: The combined mining + processing cost is $\textsf{Rs}\,40$/t, the metal price gives $\textsf{Rs}\,100$ per unit grade per tonne, and mill recovery is $0.80$. Compute the break-even cut-off grade (units).

figure.caption: Cut-off grade from cost line
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='200' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='100' x2='200' y2='40' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='30' y1='80' x2='200' y2='80' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='34' y='75' font-size='8'>cost</text><text x='110' y='140' font-size='8'>grade -> recoverable value</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At cut-off, recoverable value equals cost: $g\times\text{price}\times\text{recovery}=\text{cost}$.

**Working.**
$$g=\frac{40}{100\times0.80}=\frac{40}{80}=0.5$$

**Answer.** $0.5$ units.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: If the burden is much larger than the design value, the likely result is
options: poor fragmentation, toe and back-break with possible cratering of the collar
complete loss of all explosive energy as heat only
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Burden sets how much rock each charge must move.

**Reasoning.** Excess burden over-confines the charge: the rock breaks coarsely, leaves toe and back-break, and gases vent up the collar. (Too small a burden causes the opposite — flyrock and airblast.)

**Answer.** Poor fragmentation, toe and back-break.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A winder hoists a 10 t payload through 600 m in 60 s at steady speed. Ignoring losses and balance, the average power, in kW, is ____. Take g = 9.81 m/s².
